title: Barbosella tolimensis
taxonomy: Barbosella tolimensis is classified within the family Orchidaceae, which includes all orchids. This species is further grouped into the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Pleurothallidinae. Within the tribe Pleurothallidinae, Barbosella tolimensis is placed in the genus Barbosella. The genus Barbosella is characterized by its small, epiphytic orchids. Barbosella tolimensis is native to the tropical regions of South America.

intro: Barbosella tolimensis, also known as the Colombian Barbosella, is a species of orchid that is native to Colombia and is characterized by its small, greenish-white flowers and leathery leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. The bioactive constituents of Barbosella tolimensis include flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
